extensions: Bag and fare conditions depend on the return flight

input.reasoning:
Selected Flight 0 (AA 4366) departing at 11:22 AM - provides a comfortable mid-morning departure with reasonable arrival time at 12:49 PM, allowing the traveler to settle in on day 1. At $187 for round-trip, all flights were identically priced, so I optimized for convenience and timing.

For accommodation, selected Q4 Hotel New York City at $217 total (before taxes $180) for 3 nights. This 2-star hotel offers the best value proposition with a 3.7 rating, essential amenities (Free Wi-Fi, AC, accessible), and keeps well within budget. While Hotel 0 was slightly cheaper ($191), its very low 2.7 rating suggests poor quality. Hotel 2 (HI NYC Hostel) at $215 was comparable but Q4 offers better value as a proper hotel vs hostel.

Total cost: $187 (flight) + $217 (hotel) = $404, which is well within the $1,200 budget, leaving $796 for meals, activities, and other expenses during the 3-day trip.

No activities were requested, so none were scheduled.
input.final_message_to_user: Perfect! I've put together a great 3-day New York City trip for you that comes in at just $404 (well under your $1,200 budget). You'll fly out mid-morning on June 1st, arriving at JFK by early afternoon, giving you plenty of time to explore the city. I've booked you at the Q4 Hotel New York City, a solid 2-star hotel with great amenities including free Wi-Fi and AC. With nearly $800 remaining in your budget, you'll have plenty for dining, Broadway shows, museums, and all the amazing experiences NYC has to offer! Safe travels!
